_meta.notes: Synthesized PostToolUse stdin for a teammate's TaskUpdate(status='in_progress', owner='teammate') claiming a Task B after the lead has accepted the paired teachback Task A. This is the Bug B trigger payload: under the unfixed hook, _decide_directive's TaskUpdate branch only handles terminal-status transitions, so the pending→in_progress claim falls through to no-op and the wake-mechanism is never re-Armed (even though the prior cron entry was torn down by the eager Bug A Teardown). Fixed hook should re-Arm via _is_status_in_progress_update; idempotency lives at the skill body (CronList match), not at the hook layer. Source: derived from this session's task #20.json (backend-coder claiming the implementation Task B after teammate-Arm acceptance). NOTE: tool_response is FLAT per fixtures/wake_lifecycle/task_update_production_shape.json constraint — no statusChange.from / .to fields exist in production traffic; predicate must read tool_input.status only. 2026-05-17 (#611/#778): fixture intentionally OMITS the `agent_id` field so it remains usable for tests that exercise the lead-frame Bug B re-Arm branch (where the fixture session_id is the lead's; agent_id absence is the lead-frame signal). Tests that need the in-process teammate frame should use task_update_in_process_teammate_shape.json (which carries agent_id), or inject agent_id post-load when adapting this fixture to teammate-frame scenarios.
